title: First piece — Avant-garde wearable textile art
materials: Macrame dress overlay with cotton thread

title: hand-sculpted avant-garde wearable art in wood and silk-linen
description: I started making it and in the process I got inspired by furniture making techniques and I incorporated it into my design. This piece emerged not as a plan, but as a conversation—between hand and material, between structure and flow. Crafted in my East London studio, it fuses macramé knotting with organic wooden armature, drawing from both textile tradition and the quiet precision of joinery. The asymmetrical branch framework is carved from reclaimed wood, its form echoing the unpredictability of growth, while hand-knotted cotton netting drapes like breath held mid-exhale. Deconstructed silk-linen textiles cascade in deliberate disarray, dyed only to their natural state—cream and wood brown, unaltered, honest. Each knot, each joint, is made slowly, with attention as an act of reverence. This is wearable art not to declare, but to contemplate: a vessel for stillness, shaped by Taoist principles and the ethics of reclamation. For the collector who values the singular over the seasonal, this piece belongs not in a wardrobe, but in a life lived with intention.
etsy_tags: wearable art, avant garde fashion, hand knotted textile, slow fashion accessory, macrame sculpture, artisan crafted jewelry, sustainable luxury, east london designer, taoist inspired design, reclaimed wood accessory, linen silk fabric, one of a kind wearable, organic structure fashion

photo_direction: Photograph in soft morning light near a large window in an East London studio space, style with raw linen textiles and minimal furnishings to emphasize texture, capture from three angles: full front drape, close-up of knotwork detail, and side profile showing depth of wooden structure

attrs.material: undyed linen, found wood branches, natural jute twine, and tree bark
attrs.technique: macramé knotting, textile draping, and organic structural assembly
attrs.finish: raw, unrefined organic textures with weathered natural elements
attrs.craft_story: This piece demonstrates a sophisticated fusion of traditional fiber arts and found-object assemblage to create a dialogue between the human form and the raw structural beauty of the natural world.
